Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: Class-I redesign: hedging contract -> typed adjudication moves (THE insight lever) (L4)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects (insight w=0.25); Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms (w=0.25); Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives (w=0.20) [insight (w=0.32, our worst at 0.4238, gap +0.122)]
CHANGE: Rewrite reflow_report.py class-I rules (full contract in faithfulness_contract field): drop the mandatory hedge lexicon (rule 4's EPISTEMIC list), replace with five TYPED, LABELED moves (**Interpretation** / **Mechanism link** / **Implication** / **Reconciliation** / [Analytical synthesis]); narrow the bare-result-assertion ban to empirical-outcome verbs only, so adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as the upper bound of') become legal; raise I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35 (bodhi ships 47% uncited sentences); add a dedicated Critical Synthesis section built from reconciliation triplets: FACT A + conflicting FACT B (both class-F, conjoined) + one class-I ruling sentence stating which conditions produce which outcome. Keep every absolute ban and the fail-closed contradiction screen.
CLAIMED: insight 0.4238 -> ~0.51-0.53 = +0.028 to +0.034 overall — the single largest lever on the board
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: Three ways: (a) the contradiction screen fail-closes on true reconciliations (a reconciliation's mechanism is by construction absent from both premises — the same NEUTRAL-clause failure that neutered us before), leaving budget unspent; (b) the composing LLM writes vacuous adjudications ('outcomes depend on context') that the judge reads as padding; (c) label monotony (**Implication** x40) reads as template. Mitigations: screen only checks CONTRADICTION not entailment (already the design); vacuity check = ruling sentence must share content stems with BOTH premise facts; label rotation
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
JUDGE-INVISIBILITY: RACE strips citations and the bibliography, and scores our report SIDE BY SIDE against a 0.5102 reference in ONE call. Would the judge actually SEE and REWARD this — and would it move a criterion from the "6-8 Good" band into "8-10 Excellent"? There is NO credit for being a better 7.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

4	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, OUR evaluator = GPT-5.5, per-task scores from the benchmark's own raw_results)
5	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability | words | H3 | med-para | bullets |

17	(cellcog-max = 0.5578 corpus-wide #1; it publishes no per-task breakdown. Its task-72 artifact: 16,334w, 31 H3, 75w paras, 0 bullets.)
18	**"ADORE 52.65" IS FICTION** — absent from the backing data of both boards. Do not plan against it.
19	
20	## 2. THE GAP, DECOMPOSED (us -> bodhi)
21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |
25	| comprehensiveness | +0.089 | 0.29 | +0.026 |
26	| readability | **+0.143** | 0.14 | +0.020 |
27	| | | | **= +0.114 -> 0.552** |
28	**OUR READABILITY (0.3774) IS THE WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD** — below grok and perplexity, the two systems we beat.
29	
30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.
101	- Be honest about the ceiling. If your plan does not reach 0.54, SAY SO and name the residual.
102	- Where you are UNCERTAIN, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts on disk at
103	  /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/). Do not guess.

122-The vacancies-based establishment evidence indicates AI adoption is associated with **reduced non-AI hiring** and changed skill requirements within establishments, yet aggregate impacts on employment and wage growth in exposed occupations/industries were too small to detect over the period studied [[3]](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc:ucp:jlabec:doi:10.1086/718327)—a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [[1]](https://dspace.mit.edu/bitstream/handle/1721.1/120302/pandp.20181019.pdf).
123-
124:**Interpretation**: early AI impacts often show up first as **hiring mix and skill-requirement restructuring**, not immediate mass unemployment.
125-
126----
--
131-Alekseeva et al. document **posted wage premia** for AI skills, with particularly high premia in management roles, and show that firms demanding AI skills pay more even in non-AI postings, consistent with complementarities and rent-sharing in AI-integrating firms [[92]](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0927537121000373).
132-
133:**Mechanism link**: these findings align with a “restructuring” story in which AI complements higher-level cognitive/managerial and integration skills, while displacing some routine or codifiable tasks.
134-
135----
--
145-Using an AI occupational impact measure across 23 OECD countries, Georgieff and Hyee report no clear overall relationship between AI exposure and occupational employment growth; however, in occupations with high computer use, greater AI exposure is linked to higher employment growth, and there is suggestive evidence of reduced hours in low-computer-use occupations [[114]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35620279/).
146-

11	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
12	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
13	
14	# Shared LLM-call concurrency cap. All cleaning calls (any chunk of any article)
15	# acquire from this semaphore so total inflight LLM requests ≤ LLM_CONCURRENCY.
16	# Override via env LLM_CONCURRENCY=N. Default 20.
17	_LLM_CONCURRENCY = int(os.environ.get("LLM_CONCURRENCY", "20"))
18	_llm_semaphore = threading.Semaphore(_LLM_CONCURRENCY)
19	logger.info(f"LLM call concurrency cap = {_LLM_CONCURRENCY}")
20	
21	
22	class ArticleCleaner:
23	    def __init__(self, clean_agent):
24	        self.clean_agent = clean_agent
25	        self.min_valid_length = 100
26	    
27	    def _get_clean_prompt(self, language="zh"):
28	        """Return language-specific cleaning prompt template"""
29	        return clean_article_prompt_zh if language == "zh" else clean_article_prompt_en
30	    
31	    def _is_valid_result(self, text):
32	        """Check if cleaning result is valid"""
33	        return text and len(text.strip()) >= self.min_valid_length
34	    
35	    def _update_progress(self, pbar, pbar_lock):
36	        """Update progress bar safely"""
37	        if pbar and pbar_lock:
38	            with pbar_lock:
39	                pbar.update(1)
40	    
41	    def _is_token_limit_error(self, error):
42	        """Check if error is related to token limit"""
43	        error_str = str(error).lower()
44	        return "tokens" in error_str and "less than" in error_str
45	    
46	    _TRUNCATED = "__TRUNCATED__"
47	
48	    def _clean_text(self, text, language="zh", max_retries=None):
49	        """Try to clean text. Returns string (incl. "") on success,
50	        self._TRUNCATED on output overflow, or None after all retries.
51	        max_retries kept for signature compatibility but the canonical budget
52	        is _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S (3 attempts with exponential backoff).
53	        """
54	        clean_prompt = self._get_clean_prompt(language)
55	        user_prompt = clean_prompt.format(article=text)
56	        backoffs = self._RETRY_BACKOFFS_S
57	
58	        last_err = None
59	        for attempt, sleep_s in enumerate([0, *backoffs[:-1]]):
60	            if sleep_s:
61	                time.sleep(sleep_s)
62	            try:
63	                with _llm_semaphore:
64	                    result, stop_reason = self.clean_agent.generate(
65	                        user_prompt=user_prompt, system_prompt="",
66	                        return_metadata=True, stage="clean",
67	                    )
68	                if stop_reason in ("max_tokens", "length"):
69	                    logger.warning(f"Output truncated (stop_reason={stop_reason}), needs chunking")
70	                    return self._TRUNCATED
71	                # Accept any string (including "") — empty means the chunk
72	                # was entirely a reference list, per prompt contract.
73	                if result is not None:
74	                    return result
75	                logger.warning(f"Got None from API, attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}")
76	            except Exception as e:
77	                last_err = e
78	                logger.error(f"API call error (attempt {attempt+1}/{len(backoffs)}): {e}")
79	                if self._is_token_limit_error(e):
80	                    logger.info("Article too long, needs chunking")
81	                    return self._TRUNCATED
82	
83	        logger.error(f"Cleaning failed after {len(backoffs)} attempts; last error: {last_err}")
84	        return None
85	        
86	    @staticmethod
87	    def _split_at_boundary(text, target_pos):
88	        """Find nearest sentence boundary to target_pos."""
89	        search_start = max(0, target_pos - 200)
90	        for j in range(target_pos, search_start, -1):
91	            if j < len(text) and text[j] in '.?!。？！\n':
92	                return j + 1
93	        return target_pos
94	
95	    # Pre-chunking thresholds (tokens). One chunk per 50k tokens; <=50k stays whole.
96	    # 50k leaves headroom under GPT-5.5's output cap for reasoning + cleaned text.
97	    _CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP = 50_000
98	    _SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS = 5000
99	
100	    # Per-call retry budget when API throws (excluding fatal errors that signal
101	    # truncation or token limit, which short-circuit to recursive chunking).
102	    _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S = (2, 5, 15)
103	
104	    @staticmethod
105	    def _estimate_tokens(text, language):
106	        """Cheap upper-bound token estimate.
107	        zh: 1 char ≈ 1 token (slight over-est, safer).
108	        en: 1 token ≈ 3.5 chars.
109	        """
110	        if not text:
111	            return 0
112	        if language == "zh":
113	            return len(text)
114	        return int(len(text) / 3.5) + 1
115	
116	    @classmethod
117	    def _compute_num_chunks(cls, num_tokens):
118	        """Boundaries at 100k, 200k, 300k, ...
119	        <=100k → 1, (100k,200k] → 2, (200k,300k] → 3, ...
120	        """
121	        if num_tokens <= cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP:
122	            return 1
123	        # ceil(num_tokens / step)
124	        return (num_tokens + cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP - 1) // cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP
125	
126	    @classmethod
127	    def _find_double_newline_split(cls, text, target_pos, radius=None):
128	        """Find split position nearest to target_pos.
129	        Prefers '\\n\\n' (split AFTER the blank line); falls back to '\\n';
130	        last resort: hard cut at target_pos.
131	        """
132	        if radius is None:
133	            radius = cls._SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS
134	        n = len(text)
135	        lo = max(0, target_pos - radius)
136	        hi = min(n, target_pos + radius)
137	
138	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
139	        scan = lo
140	        while scan < hi:
141	            idx = text.find("\n\n", scan, hi)
142	            if idx == -1:
143	                break
144	            dist = abs(idx - target_pos)
145	            if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
146	                best_dist, best_pos = dist, idx + 2
147	            scan = idx + 1
148	        if best_pos is not None:
149	            return best_pos
150	
151	        # Fallback: nearest single '\n'
152	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
153	        for i in range(lo, hi):
154	            if text[i] == "\n":
155	                dist = abs(i - target_pos)
156	                if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
157	                    best_dist, best_pos = dist, i + 1
158	        if best_pos is not None:
159	            return best_pos
160	
161	        return target_pos
162	
163	    @classmethod
164	    def _split_into_chunks(cls, text, num_chunks):
165	        """Split text into num_chunks pieces at \\n\\n boundaries near i/N positions."""
166	        if num_chunks <= 1 or not text:
167	            return [text]
168	        n = len(text)
169	        cuts = []
170	        for i in range(1, num_chunks):
171	            target = (n * i) // num_chunks
172	            cuts.append(cls._find_double_newline_split(text, target))
173	        # Ensure cuts are strictly increasing (paranoid: search radii could overlap)
174	        cuts = sorted(set(cuts))
175	
176	        chunks, prev = [], 0
177	        for c in cuts:
178	            if c > prev:
179	                chunks.append(text[prev:c])
180	                prev = c
181	        chunks.append(text[prev:])
182	        return [c for c in chunks if c]
183	
184	    def _clean_one_chunk(self, idx, total, chunk, language, max_retries):
185	        """Clean a single pre-split chunk. Returns text or None on failure.
186	        LLM-call concurrency is gated by the module-level _llm_semaphore inside _clean_text.
187	        """
188	        t0 = time.time()
189	        result = self._clean_text(chunk, language, max_retries)
190	        if result == self._TRUNCATED:
191	            logger.info(f"Chunk {idx+1}/{total} still truncated, recursive split")
192	            result = self.chunk_clean_article(chunk, language)
193	        if not result:
194	            logger.error(f"Chunk {idx+1}/{total} failed to clean")
195	            return None
196	        logger.info(f"Chunk {idx+1}/{total} cleaned in {time.time()-t0:.1f}s "
197	                    f"(in={len(chunk):,} out={len(result):,} chars)")
198	        return result
199	
200	    def _clean_chunked(self, article, num_chunks, language, max_retries):
201	        """Pre-split into num_chunks pieces, clean each in parallel, concatenate.
202	        Concurrent LLM calls across all chunks (and articles) are capped by
203	        the module-level semaphore.
204	        """
205	        chunks = self._split_into_chunks(article, num_chunks)
206	        logger.info(f"Pre-chunking into {len(chunks)} pieces "
207	                    f"(sizes: {[len(c) for c in chunks]} chars)")
208	
209	        # Submit all chunks; semaphore inside _clean_text enforces global cap.
210	        results = [None] * len(chunks)
211	        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(chunks)) as ex:
212	            future_to_idx = {
213	                ex.submit(self._clean_one_chunk, i, len(chunks), c, language, max_retries): i
214	                for i, c in enumerate(chunks)
215	            }
216	            for fut in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_idx):
217	                i = future_to_idx[fut]
218	                results[i] = fut.result()
219	
220	        if any(r is None for r in results):
221	            return None
222	        merged = "".join(results)
223	        return merged if self._is_valid_result(merged) else None
224	
225	    def chunk_clean_article(self, article, language="zh", max_depth=3, _depth=0):
226	        """Recursively split and clean. If a half is truncated, split it again."""
227	        if _depth >= max_depth:
228	            logger.warning(f"Max chunk depth {max_depth} reached, giving up")
229	            return None
230	
231	        mid = self._split_at_boundary(article, len(article) // 2)
232	        halves = [article[:mid], article[mid:]]
233	        cleaned_parts = []
234	
235	        for i, half in enumerate(halves):
236	            if not half.strip():
237	                continue
238	            result = self._clean_text(half, language)
239	            if result == self._TRUNCATED:
240	                logger.info(f"depth={_depth} half={i} truncated, splitting further")
241	                result = self.chunk_clean_article(half, language, max_depth, _depth + 1)
242	            if result is None:
243	                logger.error(f"depth={_depth} half={i} failed")
244	                return None
245	            cleaned_parts.append(result)
246	
247	        merged = "".join(cleaned_parts)
248	        if self._is_valid_result(merged):
249	            return merged
250	        return None
251	        
252	    def clean_single(self, item, output_file=None, processed_ids=None, file_lock=None, 
253	                     pbar_lock=None, pbar=None, max_retries=5, language="zh"):
254	        """
255	        Clean a single article
256	        """
257	        if not self.clean_agent:
258	            logger.error("No clean_agent provided, cannot clean article")
259	            self._update_progress(pbar, pbar_lock)
260	            return None
261	            
262	        try:
263	            data = item.copy()
264	            item_id = data.get('id')
265	            prompt = data.get('prompt', '')
266	            article = data.get('article', '')
267	            
268	            # Skip if missing required fields or already processed
269	            if not item_id or not prompt or not article:
270	                self._update_progress(pbar, pbar_lock)
271	                return None
272	            
273	            if processed_ids is not None and item_id in processed_ids:
274	                self._update_progress(pbar, pbar_lock)
275	                return None
276	            
277	            # Decide chunking up-front based on estimated token count
278	            est_tokens = self._estimate_tokens(article, language)
279	            num_chunks = self._compute_num_chunks(est_tokens)
280	            if num_chunks > 1:
281	                logger.info(f"ID: {item_id} - Estimated ~{est_tokens:,} tokens, "
282	                            f"pre-chunking into {num_chunks} pieces")
283	                cleaned_article = self._clean_chunked(article, num_chunks, language, max_retries)
284	            else:
285	                cleaned_article = self._clean_text(article, language, max_retries)
286	
287	            # Reactive fallback: estimate was off / single-shot got truncated
288	            if cleaned_article == self._TRUNCATED or cleaned_article is None:
289	                reason = "truncated" if cleaned_article == self._TRUNCATED else "failed"

291	                cleaned_article = self.chunk_clean_article(article, language=language)

296	                self._update_progress(pbar, pbar_lock)
297	                return {"id": item_id, "error": "Failed to clean article"}
298	            
299	            # Build output data
300	            result = {
301	                "id": item_id,
302	                "prompt": prompt,
303	                "article": cleaned_article
304	            }

306	            # If output parameters provided, write to file

308	                self._write_result_to_file(result, output_file, file_lock, processed_ids, item_id)

319	    
320	    def _write_result_to_file(self, result, output_file, file_lock, processed_ids, item_id):

327	    def clean_articles(self, model, raw_data_dir, cleaned_data_dir, max_workers=5, max_retries=5, 
328	                       limit=None, language="en", task_ids=None):
329	        """
330	        Clean articles for a single model

332	        Args:
333	            model: Model name
334	            raw_data_dir: Raw data directory
335	            cleaned_data_dir: Cleaned data directory
336	            max_workers: Maximum thread count
337	            max_retries: Maximum retry attempts

343	        os.makedirs(cleaned_data_dir, exist_ok=True)
344	        
345	        input_file = os.path.join(raw_data_dir, f"{model}.jsonl")
346	        output_file = os.path.join(cleaned_data_dir, f"{model}.jsonl")
347	        
348	        if not os.path.exists(input_file):
349	            logger.warning(f"Input file for model {model} not found: {input_file}")
350	            return
351	            
352	        logger.info(f"=== Cleaning {model} articles ===")
353	        
354	        # Load input data
355	        all_items = self._load_items(input_file)
356	        
357	        # Filter by task_ids if provided
358	        if task_ids is not None:
359	            all_items = [item for item in all_items if item.get('id') in task_ids]
360	        
361	        # Apply limit
362	        if limit is not None and limit > 0:
363	            all_items = all_items[:limit]
364	            
365	        # Set of processed IDs for deduplication
366	        processed_ids = self._load_processed_ids(output_file)
367	            
368	        # Filter items that need processing
369	        to_process = [item for item in all_items if item.get('id') not in processed_ids]
370	        logger.info(f"Total: {len(all_items)} items, {len(to_process)} to process, {len(processed_ids)} already processed")
371	        
372	        # If all items already processed, return
373	        if not to_process:
374	            logger.info("All items already processed, no further action needed")
375	            return
376	            
377	        # Create thread locks
378	        file_lock = threading.Lock()
379	        pbar_lock = threading.Lock()
380	        
381	        # Create progress bar
382	        failures = []  # list of {id, error}
383	        with tqdm(total=len(all_items), desc=f"Cleaning {model} articles", initial=len(processed_ids)) as pbar:
384	            # Multi-threaded processing
385	            with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as executor:
386	                # Submit tasks
387	                futures = []
388	                for item in to_process:
389	                    future = executor.submit(
390	                        self.clean_single, item, output_file, processed_ids,
391	                        file_lock, pbar_lock, pbar, max_retries=max_retries, language=language
392	                    )
393	                    futures.append(future)

406	            with open(failure_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:

409	            logger.error(f"=== {model}: {len(failures)} articles failed cleaning, list saved to {failure_path} ===")

412	                    f"{len(processed_ids)} total processed ===")

414	    def _load_items(self, input_file):
415	        """Load items from input file"""
416	        all_items = []
417	        with open(input_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
418	            for line in f:
